feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Cruise ships are perfect breeding ground for viruses, but we can’t get enough
entry.summary: Buffets and shared spaces help make cruise ships ideal places for viruses to flourish, disease experts say. Still, Americans are booking cruises in record numbers.

entry.title: Is hantavirus like COVID-19? Five infectious disease experts answer some of the biggest questions about the outbreak.
entry.summary: Plus, medical experts share the virus they consider more concerning to the American public.

entry.title: Lewis Waters: Family 'heartbroken' over teen's meningitis death
entry.summary: The family of sixth form pupil Lewis Waters post a tribute following his death.

entry.title: Routine vaccines may cut dementia risk—experts have startling hypothesis on how
entry.summary: Vaccines may be training a part of our immune system long thought to be untrainable.

entry.title: This biomarker is a better predictor of heart disease than cholesterol: What to know about C-reactive protein.
entry.summary: The Conversation reports C-reactive protein is a superior heart disease risk marker over cholesterol, urging universal screening for better prevention.

entry.title: 15,000 Zombie Deer Are Eaten by Humans Each Year, and That Number is Growing
entry.summary: Learn how chronic wasting disease spreads, why infected “zombie deer” act strangely, and whether humans are at risk.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Liver When You Drink Coffee Every Day
entry.summary: Discover how coffee can support liver health by reducing the risk of fibrosis and cirrhosis, lowering enzymes and providing antioxidant protection.

entry.title: HaysMed, doctor sued for $10 million after death of patient during heart surgery
entry.summary: File photo By CRISTINA JANNEYHays PostHaysMed and one of its doctors h

entry.title: Penile implant specialist with history of far-right comments led Hantavirus presser
entry.summary: As the Trump administration sought to reassure Americans this week that a potential hantavirus outbreak posed little risk to the public, Dr. Brian Christine, one of the top public health officials in charge of infectious disease policy, stood before reporters…

entry.title: Emergency scientific consultation on Andes Virus medical countermeasures (MCM) R&D
entry.summary: The consultation will include discussions on:current epidemiological developments and transmission dynamicshistorical and recent evidence on human-to-human transmissionclinical management and natural history studiesdiagnostics, therapeutics, and vaccine MCM l…

entry.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province
entry.summary: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported.

entry.title: Africa top health body confirms new Ebola outbreak in remote Congo province
entry.summary: Africa's top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo’s remote Ituri province. The Africa CDC reported 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far. Most cases and deaths are in the Mongwalu and Rwampara health zones. The agency said that fo…

entry.title: Hantavirus case suspected in Ontario County
entry.summary: Ontario County is investigating a suspected case of locally acquired hantavirus, according to a release from Kate Ott, the county's public health director.

entry.title: A grad student’s wild idea sparks a major aging breakthrough
entry.summary: A casual conversation between graduate students helped spark a breakthrough in aging research at Mayo Clinic. Researchers discovered that tiny synthetic DNA molecules called aptamers can selectively attach to senescent “zombie cells,” which are linked to agin…

entry.title: Does the PSA test for prostate cancer save lives? New data reverse gold-standard findings
entry.summary: Health-evidence reviewers reverse recommendation on a blood test that detects a biomarker of a common cancer after decades of controversy.

entry.title: These Simple Plant Foods Are Linked to Lower Blood Pressure
entry.summary: Higher soy and legume intake may reduce the risk of high blood pressure, with the greatest benefits seen at moderate daily consumption levels.

entry.title: Common Blood Pressure Drug Supercharges Cancer Treatment in Surprising New Study
entry.summary: A common blood pressure drug may help a major class of cancer therapies work far better than expected. A widely prescribed blood pressure drug may have an unexpected second life as a cancer therapy booster. Researchers at Dartmouth Cancer Center (DCC) have di…

entry.title: Penn researchers say inconsistent GLP1 use could reduce effectiveness of weight loss
entry.summary: GLP‑1 medications remain one of the most popular weight loss drugs on the market, but new research suggests consistency may be key to maintaining their effectiveness.

entry.title: This Surprising Protein May Lower Your Alzheimer’s Risk by 27%, New Study Says
entry.summary: A new study finds that eating eggs regularly may lower your Alzheimer's risk by up to 27%. See the science-backed details and tips for your diet.

entry.title: American passenger from hantavirus-hit cruise ship speaks about life in quarantine
entry.summary: The number of people being monitored for hantavirus in the United States has grown to 41, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said Thursday.

entry.title: No clear autism link to antidepressant use during pregnancy, large study finds
entry.summary: By Nancy Lapid May 14 (Reuters) - The use of antidepressants while pregnant does not raise the children's risk for developmental disorders such as autism...

entry.title: Concerns over antidepressants and pregnancy have grown. New study offers reassurance
entry.summary: Antidepressant use before and during pregnancy has little to no association with higher odds of ADHD or autism in children, a new study has found.

entry.title: I Spiraled After a TikTok Claimed My Mattress Caused Acne
entry.summary: After years of struggling with cystic breakouts, one viral video about the Zinus bed threw me into a fit of anxiety.

entry.title: Why a Bill Gates-backed insect factory releases 30 million mosquitoes every week
entry.summary: Science News: Deep inside Medellin, Colombia, scientists are breeding millions of mosquitoes every week and then releasing them into the environment on purpose. At .

entry.title: Researchers Solve 15-Year Mystery Behind Cancer-Causing Gut Toxin
entry.summary: A new study reveals the hidden mechanism a common gut bacterium uses to damage the colon, solving a mystery that has puzzled scientists for years.

entry.title: We Now Know How Many People the CDC Is Monitoring for Hantavirus
entry.summary: There are no confirmed cases in the US, but 41 people who were potentially exposed to the Andes virus are in quarantine or being monitored for symptoms.

entry.title: Immune cells in the blood drive cognitive ageing — blocking them improves memory
entry.summary: Old T cells secrete an enzyme that can impair brain function in mice.

entry.title: 'Ballmaxxing:' Experts Say It's More Dangerous Than You Think
entry.summary: A viral new trend known as “ballmaxxing” has men injecting fluids like saline into their testicles to increase their size, but experts warn it could lead to permanent damage. Here's what you should know about this potentially dangerous trend.

entry.title: The Sleep Time Sweet Spot Is Real: Missing It Linked To Faster Aging
entry.summary: New research links both short and long sleep to faster biological aging across multiple organs and a higher risk of early death.

entry.title: PCOS, affecting 1 in 8 women worldwide, now called PMOS
entry.summary: A hormonal condition affecting 1 in 8 women around the world just got a new name in hopes of improving care. It Is now called polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome instead of polycystic ovary syndrome.

entry.title: Suspected case of hantavirus in Ontario County on Thursday
entry.summary: Ontario County Public Health (OCPH) said it's investigating a possible case of locally-acquired hantavirus. OCPH says there's no connection to the current cruise ship infections, and no risk to the general public.

entry.title: Hallucinated citations highest in social sciences preprints site
entry.summary: More than 140,000 fake citations across four research repositories were identified in papers and preprints published in 2025 alone.

entry.title: Why Weed Might Be the Next Ozempic
entry.summary: The results of a new study suggest cannabis may offer more than just “the munchies.”

entry.title: No Americans Have Hantavirus From Cruise Outbreak (Latest Updates)
entry.summary: A Dutch couple believed to have brought hantavirus aboard the ship spent months traveling in Argentina, Chile and Uruguay before boarding.

entry.title: Doctors rally behind autonomous vehicles as public health issue
entry.summary: Two high-profile doctors are urging policymakers to support AV deployment, arguing it's a public health imperative.

entry.title: CDC says there are no U.S. hantavirus cases currently, 41 people being monitored
entry.summary: The CDC said the risk to the general public from hantavirus remains low.

entry.title: Woman with boobs so big she can’t even play with her son denied surgery by NHS
entry.summary: Charlotte Innes, a woman with boobs so big she can't play with her son because she's in pain, has been denied reduction surgery five times by the NHS

entry.title: Your Eyes Could Reveal Your Risk of Osteoporosis, Study Finds
entry.summary: The eyes are a window into our deeper health.

entry.title: Brain-Stimulating Contact Lenses Match Prozac in Depression Study
entry.summary: Brain-stimulating contact lenses are as effective as Prozac at treating depression in mice via retinal pathways.

entry.title: Increase in Travel-Associated and Locally Acquired Dengue Cases — United States, 2024
entry.summary: This report describes an increase in dengue cases reported during 2024.

entry.title: The worst virus of all …
entry.summary: A cartoon by Pedro Molina.

entry.title: What happens to your brain under anesthesia?
entry.summary: New findings challenge the widespread belief that anesthesia is simply a deep sleep. The reality is more complex, researchers found.

entry.title: 2 patients at Pittsburgh VA test positive for Legionella, 11 Investigates confirms
entry.summary: Two patients at Pittsburgh’s VA Medical Center tested positive for the same bacteria that killed six patients and sickened 22 others at the hospital in 2011 and 2012.

entry.title: How to make your home smell nice without adding indoor air pollution
entry.summary: Experts share how to get that fresh feeling without overdoing it on home fragrances and the particulates they release into the air.

entry.title: “I fully expect to be at least as strong and healthy in my 90s as I am now, if not stronger”—a 72-year-old trainer shares the weekly routine she follows for strength and longevity
entry.summary: Trainer Liz Hilliard says that at 72 years old, she’s in it for the long run

entry.title: Challenging your mind can help maintain brain health. Here are steps the Alzheimer's Association recommends.
entry.summary: Challenging your mind, through games and learning new skills, may help reduce your risk of dementia, according to the Alzheimer's Association. (Sponsored by the Alzheimer's Association.)

entry.title: The political polarization of health outcomes in the USA
entry.summary: Using individual-level medical data and death records, this study finds that conservatives in the USA experienced worse health and higher mortality than liberals during the 2010s. No significant gaps in biomarkers or mortality were present before the 2010s.

entry.title: Expert issues severe warning about 'ballmaxxing' as more young men doing it
entry.summary: A health expert has warned the trend could lead to 'life-threatening sepsis'

entry.title: Man Quarantining After Hantavirus Outbreak Speaks Out: ‘I Was In A Really Dark Place’
entry.summary: Jake Rosmarin said he’s “trying not to think about” whether he’s been infected with hantavirus as he hunkers down for six weeks in a medical facility.

entry.title: How to stay safe from hantavirus, norovirus as summer travel season approaches
entry.summary: While the global threat for hantavirus remains low, there is still a chance you could be exposed to the virus while you're traveling.
